project_area: Support for adolescents and young adults (ages 14-25) in Greater Boston/Massachusetts, focusing on mental health, trauma, crisis intervention, workforce training, housing stability, and education completion
demographic_requirements: Primarily serves adolescents and young adults (ages 14-25), with priority given to high-risk populations such as youth with mental health conditions, homelessness, justice-involved youth, LGBTQ+ youth, foster care leavers, immigrant/refugee youth, neurodivergent youth, and youth needing workforce skills
eligibility_summary: Established nonprofit organizations in Greater Boston/Massachusetts that align with the grant's focus on supporting adolescents and young adults through therapeutic services, wraparound support, or pathways to independence. Organizations must demonstrate a track record and fit with 5+ of the specified factors (e.g., serving high-risk populations, addressing mental health/trauma, fostering supportive relationships).
